key_strengths: 10,000+ acres of owned, permitted salvage yards — a physical moat that took decades to build and cannot be quickly replicated
Self-reinforcing two-sided network effect connecting 170+ country buyer base with dominant insurance carrier supply
Fortress balance sheet: $5.2B net cash, zero financial debt, $1.8B annual operating cash flow
~30% ROIC (5-year average) with 34%+ net margins — among the highest sustained margins in US commercial services
Secular tailwind: total-loss frequency rising from 4% in 1980 to 22.6% in FY2025 as modern vehicles become increasingly expensive to repair
Founder Willis Johnson holds ~7.72% equity (~$4B stake) with compensation tied entirely to real stock appreciation
key_risks: Two consecutive quarters of declining insurance unit volumes (Q2 FY2026: -9% globally) — cyclical vs. structural uncertainty is the pivotal open question
Supply-side concentration: ~80% of vehicle volume from auto insurance carriers; top carriers likely represent 40-50% of supply
IAA/RB Global market share pressure acknowledged in Q2 FY2026 earnings — duopoly is not static
Bear-case valuation implies 37-50% downside from current price if volume decline proves structural
AV adoption could reduce accident frequency over a 10-year horizon, compressing the long-term volume growth assumption
red_flags: Q2 FY2026 revenue -3.6% YoY; EPS -10% — two consecutive quarterly declines unusual for Copart
CEO Jeff Liaw sold 43% of personal holdings (July 2025), reducing his stake to 31,527 shares
No price margin of safety at ~$36: stock trades near base-case intrinsic value, not at a meaningful discount
Capex ($569M FY2025) is 2.8x D&A (~$200M) — maintenance vs. growth split not disclosed, introducing owner earnings uncertainty
SBC as percentage of revenue not confirmed from available data; requires 10-K verification
buy_triggers: Stock declines to $26-30 range, creating genuine price margin of safety with base-case intrinsic value estimated at $32-37
Two to three consecutive quarters of recovering insurance unit volumes confirm the current soft patch is cyclical
Major catastrophe event (hurricane/flood season) provides volume surge demonstrating underlying leverage in the model
sell_triggers: Two or more major insurance carriers (State Farm, Allstate, GEICO, Progressive) announce material and durable volume reductions or shifts to IAA/RB Global
Net margins sustain below 25% for four consecutive quarters, indicating structural pricing pressure
Management takes on significant debt (>$2B) for an acquisition, breaking the balance-sheet-fortress principle
valuation_summary: At ~$36/share (~23x trailing P/E, ~20% below 10-year average multiple of 29x), CPRT trades near base-case intrinsic value of $32-37B; the bear case implies $18-22B (37-50% downside) and the bull case implies $50-53B (43-52% upside), making the risk/reward approximately symmetric rather than asymmetric at current levels.
source_summary: Analysis based on Copart Q1 and Q2 FY2026 earnings releases (November 2025 and March 2026), FY2025 10-K (filed September 2025), and web searches conducted March 2026; financial data current as of January 31, 2026 balance sheet date.
